questions.question: What is the key difference between `while` and `do-while`?
options: `do-while` body is guaranteed to execute at least once
questions.answer: `do-while` body is guaranteed to execute at least once

questions.question: When is the condition of a `while` loop evaluated?
questions.answer: Before each iteration

questions.question: If the condition of a `while` is initially false, how many times does the body run?
Which loop is exit-controlled?
questions.answer: `do-while`

questions.question: What is a common cause of an infinite `while` loop?
options: Variable in condition not updated inside loop
questions.answer: Variable in condition not updated inside loop

questions.question: What must end a `do-while` loop statement?
questions.answer: A semicolon `;`

questions.question: When is a `while` loop better than a `for` loop?
options: Number of iterations unknown and depends on a condition
questions.answer: Number of iterations unknown and depends on a condition

questions.question: Output of: `int i=5; do { cout<<'A'; } while(i<5);`
questions.answer: A

questions.question: Output of: `int i=5; while(i<5) { cout<<'A'; }`
questions.answer: No output

questions.question: Which loop is entry-controlled?
questions.answer: `while`

questions.question: What pattern is ideal for input validation with do-while?
options: Read in do block, check in while condition — guarantees at least one read
questions.answer: Read in do block, check in while condition — guarantees at least one read

questions.question: What does `while(true) { if(done) break; ... }` do?
options: Infinite loop with explicit break condition inside
questions.answer: Infinite loop with explicit break condition inside

questions.question: What does `continue` skip in a while loop body?
options: The rest of the current iteration body, jumps back to condition
questions.answer: The rest of the current iteration body, jumps back to condition

questions.question: Can you have `do-while(false)` run the body?
options: Yes — body runs once, then condition (false) exits
questions.answer: Yes — body runs once, then condition (false) exits

questions.question: What is a sentinel value in a while loop?
options: A special value used to terminate the loop (e.g., -1 means stop)
questions.answer: A special value used to terminate the loop (e.g., -1 means stop)

questions.question: Why can a while loop cause undefined behavior if the condition references uninitialized variables?
options: Reading uninitialized memory is undefined behavior — any value could be there
questions.answer: Reading uninitialized memory is undefined behavior — any value could be there

questions.question: What is the difference between a `while` and `for` loop in terms of power?
options: They are equally powerful — any for can be rewritten as while and vice versa
questions.answer: They are equally powerful — any for can be rewritten as while and vice versa

questions.question: What does `std::cin >> x` return that enables loop conditions like `while(cin >> x)`?
options: A reference to `cin` that is `true` on success
questions.answer: A reference to `cin` that is `true` on success

questions.question: What is an off-by-one error in a while loop?
options: Loop runs one iteration too many or too few due to wrong comparison operator
questions.answer: Loop runs one iteration too many or too few due to wrong comparison operator

questions.question: Can a `do-while` body execute with side effects before the condition is tested?
options: Yes — that's the defining feature: body always executes at least once before check
questions.answer: Yes — that's the defining feature: body always executes at least once before check

sublevels.theory:
**Loop invariants** — a logical statement that holds true before and after each loop iteration. Used to reason about loop correctness formally.

**Coroutines (C++20)** — replace complex state machines that used to be expressed with loops:
```cpp
// Generator coroutine replaces while(hasNext())
for (auto val : myGenerator()) { ... }
```

**Memory fences in concurrent loops:** in multithreaded code, loops reading shared data may need `std::atomic` or `std::memory_order` to prevent stale reads.

**Tail recursion** — some while loops can be expressed as tail-recursive functions, which compilers may optimize to the same machine code.

questions.question: What is a loop invariant?
options: A variable unchanged in the loop
A logical condition true before and after each loop iteration, used to prove correctness
A compile-time constant in the loop
questions.answer: A logical condition true before and after each loop iteration, used to prove correctness

questions.question: How do coroutines (C++20) simplify complex stateful loops?
options: Coroutine generators can yield values lazily, eliminating manual state tracking in while loops
questions.answer: Coroutine generators can yield values lazily, eliminating manual state tracking in while loops

questions.question: What is a data race in a multithreaded while loop?
options: Two loops reading the same data
Two threads reading/writing shared data without synchronization, causing undefined behavior
questions.answer: Two threads reading/writing shared data without synchronization, causing undefined behavior

questions.question: What does `std::atomic<bool> running{true};` provide in a while loop?
questions.answer: Thread-safe reads/writes of `running` across threads

questions.question: What is tail recursion and how does it relate to loops?
options: A recursive call as the last operation — compilers may optimize it into a loop (tail call optimization)
questions.answer: A recursive call as the last operation — compilers may optimize it into a loop (tail call optimization)

questions.question: In a tight computational while loop, what is 'false sharing'?
options: Multiple threads writing different variables that happen to share a cache line, causing cache invalidation
A bug where variables share the same address
questions.answer: Multiple threads writing different variables that happen to share a cache line, causing cache invalidation

questions.question: What is the time complexity of a while loop that halves its variable each iteration (e.g., `while(n>1) n/=2`)?
questions.answer: O(log n)

questions.question: What does `std::memory_order_acquire` on a load in a while loop ensure?
options: All memory operations before the corresponding release store are visible to this thread
questions.answer: All memory operations before the corresponding release store are visible to this thread

questions.question: What is 'spin-waiting' (busy-wait loop) and when might it be used?
options: A tight while loop checking a condition, used when the wait is expected to be very short to avoid OS context switch overhead
An infinite loop that's a bug
questions.answer: A tight while loop checking a condition, used when the wait is expected to be very short to avoid OS context switch overhead

questions.question: What is the advantage of `std::latch` / `std::barrier` (C++20) over a spin-wait loop?
options: OS-level blocking — threads sleep instead of burning CPU cycles while waiting
questions.answer: OS-level blocking — threads sleep instead of burning CPU cycles while waiting
